rules: Remote content never gets nodeIntegration.
contextIsolation remains true.
sandbox should be true for remote source views.
webSecurity must not be disabled.
Do not expose ipcRenderer directly through preload.
All IPC channels are named, narrow, typed, documented, and input-validated.
Capture upload endpoints use one-time expiring tokens and size/type limits.
Remote source content never receives direct access to project files, source.md dossiers, AI/provider keys, verification providers, billing context, or Git helpers.
Provider keys and billing credentials never enter the renderer, preload-exposed values, logs, patches, source dossiers, or remote views.
Cloud AI/verification requests are assembled in a trusted layer from a typed operation and policy-approved minimized context.
Local-only/private mode is enforced below the renderer UI.
The system can audit which project paths/excerpts were included in a cloud request without logging secrets.
Grounding-returned links cannot trigger automatic privileged crawling or source import.
Provider-grounded results render in a distinct component that can preserve current provider-required links/suggestions and does not grant remote content app privileges.
Local model, embedding, transcription, and autocomplete runtimes execute only through a trusted worker/sidecar boundary with explicit typed inputs.
Local runtimes never receive ambient vault access; the trusted layer passes only approved files or excerpts.
Operation budgets, cancellation, local-only policy, and external fallback approval are enforced below renderer state.
Remote source content cannot directly trigger a privileged local-model or cloud-model action.
Model files, derived indexes, and runtime caches are stored outside canonical knowledge and are integrity/version checked.
ActionTrace content capture defaults to false; raw prompts, note text, transcripts, retrieved excerpts, and outputs are not written to logs by default.
provider_privacy_requirements: send the smallest sufficient claim/excerpt rather than the whole vault
remove unrelated personal identifiers where practical
never send secrets or API keys
date provider terms/privacy/cost assumptions and recheck before production
do not claim zero retention when current provider terms state operational retention
distinguish estimated, provider-reported, and billed usage/cost
show external bytes or a privacy disclosure when measurable
do not present local external cost zero as total cost zero
